mechanism_narrative: A decline in FEDFUNDS lowers the discount rate and eases financial conditions, lifting S&P 500 prices over the next month. The signal is confirmed when rate cuts persist across consecutive monthly observations and fail if equities do not outperform after easing episodes.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive in a majority of months with falling FEDFUNDS, or if the rolling 2-year rank correlation between FEDFUNDS changes and forward returns is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above recent levels signals labor-market deterioration and weaker household income growth, which reduces equity demand and corporate revenue expectations. The effect should turn negative over the next month when unemployment accelerates materially from its recent baseline.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes show positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the average forward return after trigger events is not below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized acceleration in RSAFS and PAYEMS indicates stronger consumer spending and payroll income, supporting near-term corporate sales and positive S&P 500 returns. The signal is strongest when both series improve together rather than separately.
failure_condition: Reject if joint improvements in RSAFS and PAYEMS do not produce higher 20-day SPX returns than single-series improvements, or if the interaction term is not positive in rolling out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A rising INDPRO after a prior slowdown signals improving industrial output and inventory restocking, which should support cyclical earnings and push equities higher over the next quarter. The relationship fails when production gains are isolated from broader demand and do not translate into market breadth.
failure_condition: Reject if two-month INDPRO upswings are followed by negative 60-day SPX returns more than half the time, or if the average forward return after the trigger is not above zero.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation is rising while FEDFUNDS remains near the effective lower bound, real policy rates fall and liquidity conditions stay supportive of risk assets. This combination should predict positive equity returns over the next month and fail when inflation rises but policy rates also rise enough to offset it.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises faster than FEDFUNDS over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if periods with CPIAUCSL outpacing FEDFUNDS do not show above-average 20-day SPX gains, or if the interaction effect becomes statistically indistinguishable from zero in rolling tests.

mechanism_narrative: A fall in HOUST after a prior peak signals weakening housing demand and softer credit-sensitive consumption, which tends to precede lower S&P 500 returns over the next week. The signal is a short-horizon risk-off indicator and fails when housing weakness is already fully priced.
failure_condition: Reject if two-month declines in HOUST are not followed by negative 5-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the conditional mean return is not below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in PAYEMS and rise in UNRATE captures a genuine labor-market weakening that should depress earnings expectations and equity prices over the next quarter. The signal is stronger than either series alone because it requires both hiring loss and rising slack.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS falls and UNRATE rises over the same 2-month window
failure_condition: Reject if joint PAYEMS declines and UNRATE increases do not predict negative 60-day SPX returns, or if the joint condition adds no explanatory power beyond UNRATE alone.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in GDPC1 signals stronger broad economic demand that supports earnings growth and lifts equity valuations over the next quarter. The effect is expected to be positive when GDP growth is firming after a prior slowdown and to fail if the growth pickup is revision noise or inventory-driven only.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth turns positive versus prior quarter
failure_condition: Reject if positive quarter-over-quarter GDPC1 acceleration does not precede positive 60-day SPX returns more than 50% of the time, or if the signal disappears after excluding revision-prone quarters.
